{
  "analysis": "The venv was created and pandas/numpy/scikit-learn were successfully installed. The data files exist under /app/data/GSE193442_RAW. My previous attempt to load whole matrices was killed (likely memory limits). To avoid loading full matrices, I will run a streaming approach: for each gzipped file, extract only a small set of genes needed to identify KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ cells plus the curated ligand/receptor genes; read those subsets into pandas, concatenate, normalize based on the selected genes, and compute mean expression and LR scores. This avoids reading entire large matrices into memory. I will write a new script (/app/run_stream_analysis.py) implementing this strategy and run it with the venv Python. The outputs will be lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, and /app/answer.txt. The analysis will note the limitation that normalization uses only the selected genes (due to resource constraints).",
  "plan": "1) Write a new Python script that: (a) builds the set of required genes (markers, KIRs, LR genes), (b) for each gzipped sample uses zcat+awk to extract the header and only rows matching these genes, (c) reads the small table into pandas, prefixes cell barcodes with sample name, (d) concatenates per-sample DataFrames on the union of selected genes, (e) performs normalization per cell over the selected genes and log1p transform, (f) defines KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ populations using same heuristics, (g) computes mean expression vectors and scores curated LR pairs, (h) writes lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md and /app/answer.txt with results and intermediate counts. 2) Run the script using the venv Python. 3) Print heads of the outputs to verify.

Commands will create and run the script and then display outputs. The streaming extraction command uses awk with a regex of the selected gene names, which is efficient and memory-light.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/run_stream_analysis.py << 'PY'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Streaming analysis for DA-11-1: extract only required genes from each gzipped counts file
to avoid loading full matrices into memory. Produces lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt.
"""
import os
import glob
import subprocess
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from io import StringIO

# Files
candidates = ['/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz']
files = sorted([f for pat in candidates for f in glob.glob(pat)])
print('Found files:', files)
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Define genes of interest (markers, activation genes, KIR, LR genes)
kir_genes = ['KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2']
marker_genes = ['CD8A','CD8B','CD4']
activation_genes = ['IFNG','GZMB','PRF1','IL21','TNF','CXCL13']
lr_genes = ['FASLG','FAS','CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','TNF','TNFRSF1A','CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2','KLRK1','GZMB']
required_genes = sorted(set(kir_genes + marker_genes + activation_genes + lr_genes))
print('Required genes count:', len(required_genes))
regex = '^(' + '|'.join(required_genes) + ')$'

# Read per-file only those rows (header + selected genes) using zcat + awk
per_sample_dfs = []
for fpath in files:
    sample = os.path.basename(fpath).replace('.txt.gz','')
    # Use awk to print header and rows with gene names matching regex
    cmd = f"zcat {fpath} | awk -F'\t' -v r=\"{regex}\" 'NR==1{print; next} $1 ~ r {print}'"
    print('Running command for', sample)
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print('Error running command on', fpath, e)
        continue
    # read into pandas
    df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(out), sep='\t', header=0, index_col=0)
    # prefix columns (cell barcodes)
    df.columns = [f"{sample}__{c}" for c in df.columns]
    per_sample_dfs.append(df)
    print('Read sample', sample, 'shape (genes x cells):', df.shape)

# Union of genes (should be within required_genes)
all_genes = sorted(set().union(*[set(d.index) for d in per_sample_dfs]))
print('All genes extracted (union):', all_genes)

# Reindex each df to the union and fill missing with 0
for i in range(len(per_sample_dfs)):
    per_sample_dfs[i] = per_sample_dfs[i].reindex(all_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)

# Concatenate columns -> genes x cells
counts = pd.concat(per_sample_dfs, axis=1)
print('Concatenated counts shape (genes x cells):', counts.shape)

# transpose -> cells x genes
cells = counts.T
cells['sample'] = [c.split('__')[0] for c in cells.index]

# Basic filtering: remove cells with zero total across these selected genes
cell_sum = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
print('Total cells before filtering:', cells.shape[0])
cells = cells.loc[cell_sum > 0]
print('Total cells after removing zero-sum cells:', cells.shape[0])

# Normalize per cell using sum across selected genes and log1p
sums = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
sums[sums == 0] = 1
normed = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).div(sums, axis=0) * 1e4
logexpr = np.log1p(normed)
print('Computed log-normalized expression on selected genes, shape:', logexpr.shape)

# Heuristic cell population definitions
cd8_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD8A' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8A'] > 0.5)
if 'CD8B' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8B'] > 0.5)
kir_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for k in kir_genes:
    if k in logexpr.columns:
        kir_mask = kir_mask | (logexpr[k] > 0.0)
kir_cd8_mask = cd8_mask & kir_mask
print('KIR+CD8+ cells detected:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

cd4_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD4' in logexpr.columns:
    cd4_mask = (logexpr['CD4'] > 0.5)
act_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for a in activation_genes:
    if a in logexpr.columns:
        act_mask = act_mask | (logexpr[a] > 0.0)
pathogenic_cd4_mask = cd4_mask & act_mask
print('Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Fallback: if empty populations, relax thresholds
if kir_cd8_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('No KIR+CD8+ by heuristic; relaxing to any CD8 and any KIR raw counts >0')
    raw_counts = counts.T  # cells x genes in raw counts
    raw_cd8 = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    if 'CD8A' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw_counts['CD8A'] > 0)
    if 'CD8B' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw_counts['CD8B'] > 0)
    raw_kir = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    for k in kir_genes:
        if k in raw_counts.columns:
            raw_kir = raw_kir | (raw_counts[k] > 0)
    kir_cd8_mask = raw_cd8 & raw_kir
    print('After relaxing, KIR+CD8+ cells:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('No pathogenic CD4+ by heuristic; relaxing to any CD4 and any activation gene >0')
    raw_counts = counts.T
    raw_cd4 = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    if 'CD4' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd4 = raw_cd4 | (raw_counts['CD4'] > 0)
    raw_act = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    for a in activation_genes:
        if a in raw_counts.columns:
            raw_act = raw_act | (raw_counts[a] > 0)
    pathogenic_cd4_mask = raw_cd4 & raw_act
    print('After relaxing, pathogenic CD4+ cells:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Mean expression vectors (logexpr)
mean_kir = logexpr.loc[kir_cd8_mask].mean(axis=0) if kir_cd8_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)
mean_path = logexpr.loc[pathogenic_cd4_mask].mean(axis=0) if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)

# Curated LR pairs (non-MHC-I)
lr_pairs = [
    ('FASLG','FAS','Fas-FasL (apoptosis)'),
    ('CD274','PDCD1','PD-L1:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('PDCD1LG2','PDCD1','PD-L2:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('LGALS9','HAVCR2','Galectin-9:TIM-3 (inhibitory)'),
    ('TNF','TNFRSF1A','TNF:TNFRSF1A (inflammatory/apoptotic)'),
    ('CCL5','CCR5','CCL5:CCR5 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('CXCL9','CXCR3','CXCL9:CXCR3 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('IL10','IL10RA','IL-10:IL10R (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('TGFB1','TGFBR1','TGF-beta:TGFBR (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('MICA','KLRK1','MICA:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('MICB','KLRK1','MICB:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('ULBP1','KLRK1','ULBP1:NKG2D'),
    ('ULBP2','KLRK1','ULBP2:NKG2D'),
    ('GZMB','FAS','Granzyme B -> apoptosis (cytotoxic effector)')
]

rows = []
for lig, rec, note in lr_pairs:
    lig_mean = float(mean_kir.get(lig, 0.0))
    rec_mean = float(mean_path.get(rec, 0.0))
    score = lig_mean * rec_mean
    rows.append({'ligand': lig, 'receptor': rec, 'note': note, 'ligand_mean_logexpr': lig_mean, 'receptor_mean_logexpr': rec_mean, 'score': score})

res = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('score', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
res.to_csv('lr_results.csv', index=False)
print('Wrote lr_results.csv')

# Write trace and answer files
with open('/app/trace.md','w') as f:
    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')
    f.write('## Objective\nInfer ligand-receptor interactions between KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic gliadin-like CD4+ cells from the provided scRNA-seq matrices; produce reproducible code, intermediate counts, and ranked non-MHC-I interaction candidates.\n\n')
    f.write('## Data Sources\n')
    for p in files:
        f.write(f'- {p}: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).\n')
    f.write('\n')
    f.write('## Approach\n')
    f.write('1) Extract only required genes per file using zcat+awk to avoid loading full matrices.\n')
    f.write('2) Concatenate extracted rows across samples; normalize per cell using selected genes; log1p transform.\n')
    f.write('3) Identify KIR+CD8+ and putative pathogenic CD4+ cells by marker heuristics (thresholds described below); fallback to relaxed presence-based criteria if none found.\n')
    f.write('4) Compute mean expression and score curated LR pairs by product of means; exclude MHC-I.\n\n')
    f.write('### Required genes extracted:\n')
    f.write(', '.join(all_genes) + '\n\n')
    f.write('### Heuristics (exact):\n')
    f.write('- KIR+CD8+: (logexpr CD8A>0.5 or CD8B>0.5) AND any KIR gene logexpr>0.\n')
    f.write('- Pathogenic CD4+: (logexpr CD4>0.5) AND any activation gene (IFNG,GZMB,PRF1,IL21,TNF,CXCL13) detected.\n')
    f.write('\n### Quantitative intermediate results (this run):\n')
    f.write(f'- Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'- Extracted genes: {len(all_genes)}\n')
    f.write(f"- Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {logexpr.shape[0]}\n")
    f.write(f"- KIR+CD8+ cells detected: {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write(f"- Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected: {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write('\n### Limitations\n')
    f.write('- To avoid memory limits, normalization used only the selected gene set rather than genome-wide counts; this can bias relative expression magnitudes.\n')
    f.write('- Population definitions are heuristic and lack tetramer/TCR confirmation of gliadin-specificity.\n')
    f.write('- Scoring is a simple product of mean log-expressions and lacks statistical testing.\n')
    f.write('\n## Results\nTop LR candidates saved in lr_results.csv (ranked by product of mean log-expression).\n')
    f.write('\n## References\n- Nagata S. (1997). Apoptosis by death factor. Cell. DOI:10.1016/S0092-8674(00)81849-5\n- Voskoboinik I. et al. (2015). Perforin and granzymes. Nat Rev Immunol. DOI:10.1038/nri3839\n')

with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('Predicted top non-MHC-I signaling pathways mediating KIR+CD8+ T cell suppression and cytotoxic targeting of pathogenic gliadin-reactive CD4+ T cells:\n\n')
    top = res[res['score']>0].head(8)
    if top.empty:
        f.write('No non-zero scoring ligand-receptor pairs were detected with the curated list and heuristic definitions.\n')
    else:
        for _, row in top.iterrows():
            f.write(f"- {row['note']} (ligand {row['ligand']} in KIR+CD8+; receptor {row['receptor']} in pathogenic CD4+). Score={row['score']:.4g}\n")
    f.write('\nInterpretation: Top candidates typically include death-receptor mediated apoptosis (FasL-Fas; granzyme activity), NKG2D stress-ligand interactions (MICA/MICB/ULBPs), inhibitory checkpoints (PD-L1/PD-1, Galectin-9/TIM-3), immunosuppressive cytokines (IL-10, TGF-beta), and chemokine-mediated interactions (CCL5-CCR5, CXCL9-CXCR3). These are mechanistic hypotheses consistent with cytotoxic and suppressive roles and should be validated experimentally.\n')

print('Finished streaming analysis; outputs: lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt')

#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Streaming analysis for DA-11-1: extract only required genes from each gzipped counts file
to avoid loading full matrices into memory. Produces lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt.
"""
import os
import glob
import subprocess
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from io import StringIO

# Files
candidates = ['/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz']
files = sorted([f for pat in candidates for f in glob.glob(pat)])
print('Found files:', files)
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Define genes of interest (markers, activation genes, KIR, LR genes)
kir_genes = ['KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2']
marker_genes = ['CD8A','CD8B','CD4']
activation_genes = ['IFNG','GZMB','PRF1','IL21','TNF','CXCL13']
lr_genes = ['FASLG','FAS','CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','TNF','TNFRSF1A','CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2','KLRK1','GZMB']
required_genes = sorted(set(kir_genes + marker_genes + activation_genes + lr_genes))
print('Required genes count:', len(required_genes))
regex = '^(' + '|'.join(required_genes) + ')$'

# Read per-file only those rows (header + selected genes) using zcat + awk
per_sample_dfs = []
for fpath in files:
    sample = os.path.basename(fpath).replace('.txt.gz','')
    # Use awk to print header and rows with gene names matching regex
    cmd = f"zcat {fpath} | awk -F'\t' -v r=\"{regex}\" 'NR==1{print; next} $1 ~ r {print}'"
    print('Running command for', sample)
    try:
        out = subprocess.check_output(cmd, shell=True, text=True)
    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
        print('Error running command on', fpath, e)
        continue
    # read into pandas
    df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(out), sep='\t', header=0, index_col=0)
    # prefix columns (cell barcodes)
    df.columns = [f"{sample}__{c}" for c in df.columns]
    per_sample_dfs.append(df)
    print('Read sample', sample, 'shape (genes x cells):', df.shape)

# Union of genes (should be within required_genes)
all_genes = sorted(set().union(*[set(d.index) for d in per_sample_dfs]))
print('All genes extracted (union):', all_genes)

# Reindex each df to the union and fill missing with 0
for i in range(len(per_sample_dfs)):
    per_sample_dfs[i] = per_sample_dfs[i].reindex(all_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)

# Concatenate columns -> genes x cells
counts = pd.concat(per_sample_dfs, axis=1)
print('Concatenated counts shape (genes x cells):', counts.shape)

# transpose -> cells x genes
cells = counts.T
cells['sample'] = [c.split('__')[0] for c in cells.index]

# Basic filtering: remove cells with zero total across these selected genes
cell_sum = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
print('Total cells before filtering:', cells.shape[0])
cells = cells.loc[cell_sum > 0]
print('Total cells after removing zero-sum cells:', cells.shape[0])

# Normalize per cell using sum across selected genes and log1p
sums = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
sums[sums == 0] = 1
normed = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).div(sums, axis=0) * 1e4
logexpr = np.log1p(normed)
print('Computed log-normalized expression on selected genes, shape:', logexpr.shape)

# Heuristic cell population definitions
cd8_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD8A' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8A'] > 0.5)
if 'CD8B' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8B'] > 0.5)
kir_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for k in kir_genes:
    if k in logexpr.columns:
        kir_mask = kir_mask | (logexpr[k] > 0.0)
kir_cd8_mask = cd8_mask & kir_mask
print('KIR+CD8+ cells detected:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

cd4_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD4' in logexpr.columns:
    cd4_mask = (logexpr['CD4'] > 0.5)
act_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for a in activation_genes:
    if a in logexpr.columns:
        act_mask = act_mask | (logexpr[a] > 0.0)
pathogenic_cd4_mask = cd4_mask & act_mask
print('Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Fallback: if empty populations, relax thresholds
if kir_cd8_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('No KIR+CD8+ by heuristic; relaxing to any CD8 and any KIR raw counts >0')
    raw_counts = counts.T  # cells x genes in raw counts
    raw_cd8 = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    if 'CD8A' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw_counts['CD8A'] > 0)
    if 'CD8B' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw_counts['CD8B'] > 0)
    raw_kir = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    for k in kir_genes:
        if k in raw_counts.columns:
            raw_kir = raw_kir | (raw_counts[k] > 0)
    kir_cd8_mask = raw_cd8 & raw_kir
    print('After relaxing, KIR+CD8+ cells:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('No pathogenic CD4+ by heuristic; relaxing to any CD4 and any activation gene >0')
    raw_counts = counts.T
    raw_cd4 = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    if 'CD4' in raw_counts.columns:
        raw_cd4 = raw_cd4 | (raw_counts['CD4'] > 0)
    raw_act = pd.Series(False, index=raw_counts.index)
    for a in activation_genes:
        if a in raw_counts.columns:
            raw_act = raw_act | (raw_counts[a] > 0)
    pathogenic_cd4_mask = raw_cd4 & raw_act
    print('After relaxing, pathogenic CD4+ cells:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Mean expression vectors (logexpr)
mean_kir = logexpr.loc[kir_cd8_mask].mean(axis=0) if kir_cd8_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)
mean_path = logexpr.loc[pathogenic_cd4_mask].mean(axis=0) if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)

# Curated LR pairs (non-MHC-I)
lr_pairs = [
    ('FASLG','FAS','Fas-FasL (apoptosis)'),
    ('CD274','PDCD1','PD-L1:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('PDCD1LG2','PDCD1','PD-L2:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('LGALS9','HAVCR2','Galectin-9:TIM-3 (inhibitory)'),
    ('TNF','TNFRSF1A','TNF:TNFRSF1A (inflammatory/apoptotic)'),
    ('CCL5','CCR5','CCL5:CCR5 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('CXCL9','CXCR3','CXCL9:CXCR3 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('IL10','IL10RA','IL-10:IL10R (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('TGFB1','TGFBR1','TGF-beta:TGFBR (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('MICA','KLRK1','MICA:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('MICB','KLRK1','MICB:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('ULBP1','KLRK1','ULBP1:NKG2D'),
    ('ULBP2','KLRK1','ULBP2:NKG2D'),
    ('GZMB','FAS','Granzyme B -> apoptosis (cytotoxic effector)')
]

rows = []
for lig, rec, note in lr_pairs:
    lig_mean = float(mean_kir.get(lig, 0.0))
    rec_mean = float(mean_path.get(rec, 0.0))
    score = lig_mean * rec_mean
    rows.append({'ligand': lig, 'receptor': rec, 'note': note, 'ligand_mean_logexpr': lig_mean, 'receptor_mean_logexpr': rec_mean, 'score': score})

res = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('score', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
res.to_csv('lr_results.csv', index=False)
print('Wrote lr_results.csv')

# Write trace and answer files
with open('/app/trace.md','w') as f:
    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')
    f.write('## Objective\nInfer ligand-receptor interactions between KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic gliadin-like CD4+ cells from the provided scRNA-seq matrices; produce reproducible code, intermediate counts, and ranked non-MHC-I interaction candidates.\n\n')
    f.write('## Data Sources\n')
    for p in files:
        f.write(f'- {p}: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).\n')
    f.write('\n')
    f.write('## Approach\n')
    f.write('1) Extract only required genes per file using zcat+awk to avoid loading full matrices.\n')
    f.write('2) Concatenate extracted rows across samples; normalize per cell using selected genes; log1p transform.\n')
    f.write('3) Identify KIR+CD8+ and putative pathogenic CD4+ cells by marker heuristics (thresholds described below); fallback to relaxed presence-based criteria if none found.\n')
    f.write('4) Compute mean expression and score curated LR pairs by product of means; exclude MHC-I.\n\n')
    f.write('### Required genes extracted:\n')
    f.write(', '.join(all_genes) + '\n\n')
    f.write('### Heuristics (exact):\n')
    f.write('- KIR+CD8+: (logexpr CD8A>0.5 or CD8B>0.5) AND any KIR gene logexpr>0.\n')
    f.write('- Pathogenic CD4+: (logexpr CD4>0.5) AND any activation gene (IFNG,GZMB,PRF1,IL21,TNF,CXCL13) detected.\n')
    f.write('\n### Quantitative intermediate results (this run):\n')
    f.write(f'- Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'- Extracted genes: {len(all_genes)}\n')
    f.write(f"- Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {logexpr.shape[0]}\n")
    f.write(f"- KIR+CD8+ cells detected: {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write(f"- Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected: {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write('\n### Limitations\n')
    f.write('- To avoid memory limits, normalization used only the selected gene set rather than genome-wide counts; this can bias relative expression magnitudes.\n')
    f.write('- Population definitions are heuristic and lack tetramer/TCR confirmation of gliadin-specificity.\n')
    f.write('- Scoring is a simple product of mean log-expressions and lacks statistical testing.\n')
    f.write('\n## Results\nTop LR candidates saved in lr_results.csv (ranked by product of mean log-expression).\n')
    f.write('\n## References\n- Nagata S. (1997). Apoptosis by death factor. Cell. DOI:10.1016/S0092-8674(00)81849-5\n- Voskoboinik I. et al. (2015). Perforin and granzymes. Nat Rev Immunol. DOI:10.1038/nri3839\n')

with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:

content.text:
{
  "analysis": "The previous streaming script failed due to an f-string syntax error when embedding awk code containing braces. The environment has a Python venv with pandas/numpy/scikit-learn installed and the raw gzipped count files are present at /app/data/GSE193442_RAW. The earlier full-read approach was killed (memory), so a streaming approach that reads only necessary genes is required. I will replace the broken script with a pure-Python streaming implementation that uses gzip.open to read each file line-by-line, selects only rows whose gene name is in a predefined small required set, builds per-sample DataFrames, concatenates them, normalizes over the selected genes, defines KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ populations by heuristics (with relaxed fallback), computes mean expression and scores curated non-MHC-I LR pairs, and writes lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, and /app/answer.txt. This avoids shell quoting issues and is memory-light because we only retain a small number of gene rows per file.",
  "plan": "1) Overwrite /app/run_stream_analysis.py with a corrected script that streams gzipped files using gzip.open and selects only required genes. 2) Run the script with the venv Python (/app/venv/bin/python). 3) Print the generated outputs (lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md head, /app/answer.txt). The script will print intermediate counts (files found, required genes extracted, number of cells after filtering, number of KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ cells).",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/run_stream_analysis.py << 'PY'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Streaming analysis for DA-11-1: pure-Python extraction of required genes from gzipped count files.
Writes lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt.
"""
import os
import glob
import gzip
from io import StringIO
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# locate files
files = sorted(glob.glob('/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz'))
print('Found files:', len(files))
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Genes of interest
kir_genes = ['KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2']
marker_genes = ['CD8A','CD8B','CD4']
activation_genes = ['IFNG','GZMB','PRF1','IL21','TNF','CXCL13']
lr_genes = ['FASLG','FAS','CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','TNF','TNFRSF1A','CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2','KLRK1','GZMB']
required_genes = sorted(set(kir_genes + marker_genes + activation_genes + lr_genes))
print('Required genes count:', len(required_genes))
required_set = set(required_genes)

per_sample_dfs = []
all_gene_names_seen = set()
for fpath in files:
    sample = os.path.basename(fpath).replace('.txt.gz','')
    print('Processing', sample)
    with gzip.open(fpath, 'rt') as fh:
        # read header line for cell barcodes
        header = fh.readline().rstrip('\n').split('\t')
        # header[0] is gene name column
        barcodes = header[1:]
        # prepare storage for selected rows
        rows = []
        genes_idx = []
        for line in fh:
            # split only at first tab to test gene name quickly
            parts = line.split('\t', 1)
            if not parts:
                continue
            gene = parts[0]
            if gene in required_set:
                # now split remaining into values
                rest = parts[1].rstrip('\n')
                vals = rest.split('\t')
                # ensure consistent length
                if len(vals) != len(barcodes):
                    # skip malformed line
                    continue
                rows.append(vals)
                genes_idx.append(gene)
                all_gene_names_seen.add(gene)
    if not rows:
        print('No required genes found in', sample)
        # create empty DataFrame with required genes but zero columns
        df = pd.DataFrame(index=required_genes)
    else:
        # create DataFrame genes x cells
        mat = pd.DataFrame(rows, index=genes_idx, columns=barcodes).astype(int)
        # reindex to unique genes (if duplicates, keep first by grouping)
        mat = mat.groupby(mat.index).first()
        # prefix columns with sample name
        mat.columns = [f"{sample}__{c}" for c in mat.columns]
        # reindex to required_genes to ensure consistent ordering
        mat = mat.reindex(required_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)
        df = mat
    print('Sample', sample, 'extracted shape (genes x cells):', df.shape)
    per_sample_dfs.append(df)

# Union of genes actually present (subset of required_genes)
all_genes = required_genes  # we explicitly reindexed to required_genes above
# Concatenate genes x cells across samples
counts = pd.concat(per_sample_dfs, axis=1)
print('Concatenated counts shape (genes x cells):', counts.shape)

# Transpose to cells x genes
cells = counts.T
cells['sample'] = [c.split('__')[0] for c in cells.index]

# Remove cells with zero sum across these selected genes
nonzero_sum = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
print('Cells before filtering:', cells.shape[0])
cells = cells.loc[nonzero_sum > 0]
print('Cells after removing zero-sum cells:', cells.shape[0])

# Normalize per cell (over selected genes) and log1p
sums = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
sums[sums == 0] = 1
normed = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).div(sums, axis=0) * 1e4
logexpr = np.log1p(normed)
print('Logexpr shape:', logexpr.shape)

# Heuristic population definitions
cd8_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD8A' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8A'] > 0.5)
if 'CD8B' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8B'] > 0.5)
kir_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for k in kir_genes:
    if k in logexpr.columns:
        kir_mask = kir_mask | (logexpr[k] > 0.0)
kir_cd8_mask = cd8_mask & kir_mask
print('KIR+CD8+ cells detected (heuristic):', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

cd4_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD4' in logexpr.columns:
    cd4_mask = (logexpr['CD4'] > 0.5)
act_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for a in activation_genes:
    if a in logexpr.columns:
        act_mask = act_mask | (logexpr[a] > 0.0)
pathogenic_cd4_mask = cd4_mask & act_mask
print('Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected (heuristic):', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Relaxed fallback if empty
if kir_cd8_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('Relaxing KIR+CD8+ criteria to presence-based on raw counts')
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd8 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD8A' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8A'] > 0)
    if 'CD8B' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8B'] > 0)
    raw_kir = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for k in kir_genes:
        if k in raw.columns:
            raw_kir = raw_kir | (raw[k] > 0)
    kir_cd8_mask = raw_cd8 & raw_kir
    print('After relaxing, KIR+CD8+ count:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('Relaxing pathogenic CD4+ criteria to presence-based on raw counts')
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd4 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD4' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd4 = raw_cd4 | (raw['CD4'] > 0)
    raw_act = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for a in activation_genes:
        if a in raw.columns:
            raw_act = raw_act | (raw[a] > 0)
    pathogenic_cd4_mask = raw_cd4 & raw_act
    print('After relaxing, pathogenic CD4+ count:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Compute mean log-expression vectors
mean_kir = logexpr.loc[kir_cd8_mask].mean(axis=0) if kir_cd8_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)
mean_path = logexpr.loc[pathogenic_cd4_mask].mean(axis=0) if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)

# Curated LR pairs (non-MHC-I)
lr_pairs = [
    ('FASLG','FAS','Fas-FasL (apoptosis)'),
    ('CD274','PDCD1','PD-L1:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('PDCD1LG2','PDCD1','PD-L2:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('LGALS9','HAVCR2','Galectin-9:TIM-3 (inhibitory)'),
    ('TNF','TNFRSF1A','TNF:TNFRSF1A (inflammatory/apoptotic)'),
    ('CCL5','CCR5','CCL5:CCR5 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('CXCL9','CXCR3','CXCL9:CXCR3 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('IL10','IL10RA','IL-10:IL10R (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('TGFB1','TGFBR1','TGF-beta:TGFBR (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('MICA','KLRK1','MICA:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('MICB','KLRK1','MICB:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('ULBP1','KLRK1','ULBP1:NKG2D'),
    ('ULBP2','KLRK1','ULBP2:NKG2D'),
    ('GZMB','FAS','Granzyme B -> apoptosis (cytotoxic effector)')
]

rows = []
for lig, rec, note in lr_pairs:
    lig_mean = float(mean_kir.get(lig, 0.0))
    rec_mean = float(mean_path.get(rec, 0.0))
    score = lig_mean * rec_mean
    rows.append({'ligand': lig, 'receptor': rec, 'note': note, 'ligand_mean_logexpr': lig_mean, 'receptor_mean_logexpr': rec_mean, 'score': score})

res = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('score', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
res.to_csv('lr_results.csv', index=False)
print('Wrote lr_results.csv')

# Write trace.md
with open('/app/trace.md','w') as f:
    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')
    f.write('## Objective\nInfer ligand-receptor interactions between KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic gliadin-like CD4+ cells from the provided scRNA-seq matrices; produce reproducible code, intermediate counts, and ranked non-MHC-I interaction candidates.\n\n')
    f.write('## Data Sources\n')
    for p in files:
        f.write(f'- {p}: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).\n')
    f.write('\n## Approach\n')
    f.write('1) Extracted only required genes (listed below) using pure-Python streaming over gzipped files to avoid memory blowup.\n')
    f.write('2) Concatenated extracted rows across samples; normalized per cell using selected genes; log1p transformed.\n')
    f.write('3) Identified KIR+CD8+ and putative pathogenic CD4+ cells using marker heuristics; used relaxed presence-based fallback if none found.\n')
    f.write('4) Computed mean expression and scored curated non-MHC-I LR pairs by product of means.\n\n')
    f.write('### Required genes extracted:\n')
    f.write(', '.join(required_genes) + '\n\n')
    f.write('### Heuristics (exact):\n')
    f.write('- KIR+CD8+: (logexpr CD8A>0.5 or CD8B>0.5) AND any KIR gene logexpr>0.\n')
    f.write('- Pathogenic CD4+: (logexpr CD4>0.5) AND any activation gene (IFNG,GZMB,PRF1,IL21,TNF,CXCL13) detected.\n\n')
    f.write('### Quantitative intermediate results (this run):\n')
    f.write(f'- Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'- Extracted genes: {len(all_genes)}\n')
    f.write(f"- Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {logexpr.shape[0]}\n")
    f.write(f"- KIR+CD8+ cells detected: {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write(f"- Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected: {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write('\n### Limitations\n')
    f.write('- To avoid memory limits, normalization used only the selected gene set rather than genome-wide counts; this can bias expression magnitudes.\n')
    f.write('- Population definitions are heuristic and lack tetramer/TCR confirmation of gliadin-specificity.\n')
    f.write('- Scoring is a simple product of mean log-expressions and lacks statistical testing.\n')
    f.write('\n## Results\nTop LR candidates saved in lr_results.csv (ranked by product of mean log-expression).\n')
    f.write('\n## References\n- Nagata S. (1997). Apoptosis by death factor. Cell. DOI:10.1016/S0092-8674(00)81849-5\n- Voskoboinik I. et al. (2015). Perforin and granzymes. Nat Rev Immunol. DOI:10.1038/nri3839\n')

# Write answer.txt
with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('Predicted top non-MHC-I signaling pathways mediating KIR+CD8+ T cell suppression and cytotoxic targeting of pathogenic gliadin-reactive CD4+ T cells:\n\n')
    top = res[res['score']>0].head(8)
    if top.empty:
        f.write('No non-zero scoring ligand-receptor pairs were detected with the curated list and heuristic definitions.\n')
    else:
        for _, row in top.iterrows():
            f.write(f"- {row['note']} (ligand {row['ligand']} in KIR+CD8+; receptor {row['receptor']} in pathogenic CD4+). Score={row['score']:.4g}\n")
    f.write('\nInterpretation: Top candidates typically include death-receptor mediated apoptosis (FasL-Fas; granzyme activity), NKG2D stress-ligand interactions (MICA/MICB/ULBPs), immune checkpoints (PD-L1/PD-1, Galectin-9/TIM-3), immunosuppressive cytokines (IL-10, TGF-beta), and chemokine-mediated interactions (CCL5-CCR5, CXCL9-CXCR3). These are mechanistic hypotheses consistent with cytotoxic and suppressive roles and should be validated experimentally.\n')

print('Finished streaming analysis; outputs: lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt')
PY
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "/app/venv/bin/python /app/run_stream_analysis.py
",

    {
      "keystrokes": "echo '----- lr_results.csv -----'
",

    {

",

",

                    # skip malformed line
                    continue
                rows.append(vals)
                genes_idx.append(gene)
                all_gene_names_seen.add(gene)
    if not rows:
        print('No required genes found in', sample)
        # create empty DataFrame with required genes but zero columns
        df = pd.DataFrame(index=required_genes)
    else:
        # create DataFrame genes x cells
        mat = pd.DataFrame(rows, index=genes_idx, columns=barcodes).astype(int)
        # reindex to unique genes (if duplicates, keep first by grouping)
        mat = mat.groupby(mat.index).first()
        # prefix columns with sample name
        mat.columns = [f"{sample}__{c}" for c in mat.columns]
        # reindex to required_genes to ensure consistent ordering
        mat = mat.reindex(required_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)
        df = mat
    print('Sample', sample, 'extracted shape (genes x cells):', df.shape)
    per_sample_dfs.append(df)

# Union of genes actually present (subset of required_genes)
all_genes = required_genes  # we explicitly reindexed to required_genes above
# Concatenate genes x cells across samples
counts = pd.concat(per_sample_dfs, axis=1)
print('Concatenated counts shape (genes x cells):', counts.shape)

# Transpose to cells x genes
cells = counts.T
cells['sample'] = [c.split('__')[0] for c in cells.index]

# Remove cells with zero sum across these selected genes
nonzero_sum = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
print('Cells before filtering:', cells.shape[0])
cells = cells.loc[nonzero_sum > 0]
print('Cells after removing zero-sum cells:', cells.shape[0])

# Normalize per cell (over selected genes) and log1p
sums = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
sums[sums == 0] = 1
normed = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).div(sums, axis=0) * 1e4
logexpr = np.log1p(normed)
print('Logexpr shape:', logexpr.shape)

# Heuristic population definitions
cd8_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD8A' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8A'] > 0.5)
if 'CD8B' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8B'] > 0.5)
kir_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for k in kir_genes:
    if k in logexpr.columns:
        kir_mask = kir_mask | (logexpr[k] > 0.0)
kir_cd8_mask = cd8_mask & kir_mask
print('KIR+CD8+ cells detected (heuristic):', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

cd4_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD4' in logexpr.columns:
    cd4_mask = (logexpr['CD4'] > 0.5)
act_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for a in activation_genes:
    if a in logexpr.columns:
        act_mask = act_mask | (logexpr[a] > 0.0)
pathogenic_cd4_mask = cd4_mask & act_mask
print('Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected (heuristic):', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Relaxed fallback if empty
if kir_cd8_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('Relaxing KIR+CD8+ criteria to presence-based on raw counts')
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd8 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD8A' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8A'] > 0)
    if 'CD8B' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8B'] > 0)
    raw_kir = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for k in kir_genes:
        if k in raw.columns:
            raw_kir = raw_kir | (raw[k] > 0)
    kir_cd8_mask = raw_cd8 & raw_kir
    print('After relaxing, KIR+CD8+ count:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() == 0:
    print('Relaxing pathogenic CD4+ criteria to presence-based on raw counts')
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd4 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD4' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd4 = raw_cd4 | (raw['CD4'] > 0)
    raw_act = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for a in activation_genes:
        if a in raw.columns:
            raw_act = raw_act | (raw[a] > 0)
    pathogenic_cd4_mask = raw_cd4 & raw_act
    print('After relaxing, pathogenic CD4+ count:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# Compute mean log-expression vectors
mean_kir = logexpr.loc[kir_cd8_mask].mean(axis=0) if kir_cd8_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)
mean_path = logexpr.loc[pathogenic_cd4_mask].mean(axis=0) if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() > 0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)

# Curated LR pairs (non-MHC-I)
lr_pairs = [
    ('FASLG','FAS','Fas-FasL (apoptosis)'),
    ('CD274','PDCD1','PD-L1:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('PDCD1LG2','PDCD1','PD-L2:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('LGALS9','HAVCR2','Galectin-9:TIM-3 (inhibitory)'),
    ('TNF','TNFRSF1A','TNF:TNFRSF1A (inflammatory/apoptotic)'),
    ('CCL5','CCR5','CCL5:CCR5 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('CXCL9','CXCR3','CXCL9:CXCR3 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('IL10','IL10RA','IL-10:IL10R (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('TGFB1','TGFBR1','TGF-beta:TGFBR (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('MICA','KLRK1','MICA:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('MICB','KLRK1','MICB:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('ULBP1','KLRK1','ULBP1:NKG2D'),
    ('ULBP2','KLRK1','ULBP2:NKG2D'),
    ('GZMB','FAS','Granzyme B -> apoptosis (cytotoxic effector)')
]

rows = []
for lig, rec, note in lr_pairs:
    lig_mean = float(mean_kir.get(lig, 0.0))
    rec_mean = float(mean_path.get(rec, 0.0))
    score = lig_mean * rec_mean
    rows.append({'ligand': lig, 'receptor': rec, 'note': note, 'ligand_mean_logexpr': lig_mean, 'receptor_mean_logexpr': rec_mean, 'score': score})

res = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('score', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
res.to_csv('lr_results.csv', index=False)
print('Wrote lr_results.csv')

# Write trace.md
with open('/app/trace.md','w') as f:
    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')
    f.write('## Objective\nInfer ligand-receptor interactions between KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic gliadin-like CD4+ cells from the provided scRNA-seq matrices; produce reproducible code, intermediate counts, and ranked non-MHC-I interaction candidates.\n\n')
    f.write('## Data Sources\n')
    for p in files:
        f.write(f'- {p}: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).\n')
    f.write('\n## Approach\n')
    f.write('1) Extracted only required genes (listed below) using pure-Python streaming over gzipped files to avoid memory blowup.\n')
    f.write('2) Concatenated extracted rows across samples; normalized per cell using selected genes; log1p transformed.\n')
    f.write('3) Identified KIR+CD8+ and putative pathogenic CD4+ cells using marker heuristics; used relaxed presence-based fallback if none found.\n')
    f.write('4) Computed mean expression and scored curated non-MHC-I LR pairs by product of means.\n\n')
    f.write('### Required genes extracted:\n')
    f.write(', '.join(required_genes) + '\n\n')
    f.write('### Heuristics (exact):\n')
    f.write('- KIR+CD8+: (logexpr CD8A>0.5 or CD8B>0.5) AND any KIR gene logexpr>0.\n')
    f.write('- Pathogenic CD4+: (logexpr CD4>0.5) AND any activation gene (IFNG,GZMB,PRF1,IL21,TNF,CXCL13) detected.\n\n')
    f.write('### Quantitative intermediate results (this run):\n')
    f.write(f'- Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'- Extracted genes: {len(all_genes)}\n')
    f.write(f"- Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {logexpr.shape[0]}\n")
    f.write(f"- KIR+CD8+ cells detected: {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write(f"- Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected: {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n")
    f.write('\n### Limitations\n')
    f.write('- To avoid memory limits, normalization used only the selected gene set rather than genome-wide counts; this can bias expression magnitudes.\n')
    f.write('- Population definitions are heuristic and lack tetramer/TCR confirmation of gliadin-specificity.\n')
    f.write('- Scoring is a simple product of mean log-expressions and lacks statistical testing.\n')
    f.write('\n## Results\nTop LR candidates saved in lr_results.csv (ranked by product of mean log-expression).\n')
    f.write('\n## References\n- Nagata S. (1997). Apoptosis by death factor. Cell. DOI:10.1016/S0092-8674(00)81849-5\n- Voskoboinik I. et al. (2015). Perforin and granzymes. Nat Rev Immunol. DOI:10.1038/nri3839\n')

# Write answer.txt
with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('Predicted top non-MHC-I signaling pathways mediating KIR+CD8+ T cell suppression and cytotoxic targeting of pathogenic gliadin-reactive CD4+ T cells:\n\n')
    top = res[res['score']>0].head(8)
    if top.empty:
        f.write('No non-zero scoring ligand-receptor pairs were detected with the curated list and heuristic definitions.\n')
    else:
        for _, row in top.iterrows():
            f.write(f"- {row['note']} (ligand {row['ligand']} in KIR+CD8+; receptor {row['receptor']} in pathogenic CD4+). Score={row['score']:.4g}\n")
    f.write('\nInterpretation: Top candidates typically include death-receptor mediated apoptosis (FasL-Fas; granzyme activity), NKG2D stress-ligand interactions (MICA/MICB/ULBPs), immune checkpoints (PD-L1/PD-1, Galectin-9/TIM-3), immunosuppressive cytokines (IL-10, TGF-beta), and chemokine-mediated interactions (CCL5-CCR5, CXCL9-CXCR3). These are mechanistic hypotheses consistent with cytotoxic and suppressive roles and should be validated experimentally.\n')

print('Finished streaming analysis; outputs: lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt')

# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1

## Objective
Infer ligand-receptor interactions between KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic gliadin-like CD4+ cells from the provided scRNA-seq matrices; produce reproducible code, inte
rmediate counts, and ranked non-MHC-I interaction candidates.

## Data Sources
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820724_MS01092019_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820725_MS08162018_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820726_MS09102018_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820727_MS11132018_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820728_MS658_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820729_MS9020_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820730_HC11_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820731_HC12_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820732_HC13_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).
- /app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820733_HC14_counts.txt.gz: gzipped TSV (genes x cells).

## Approach
1) Extracted only required genes (listed below) using pure-Python streaming over gzipped files to avoid memory blowup.
2) Concatenated extracted rows across samples; normalized per cell using selected genes; log1p transformed.
3) Identified KIR+CD8+ and putative pathogenic CD4+ cells using marker heuristics; used relaxed presence-based fallback if none found.
4) Computed mean expression and scored curated non-MHC-I LR pairs by product of means.

### Required genes extracted:
CCL5, CCR5, CD274, CD4, CD8A, CD8B, CXCL13, CXCL9, CXCR3, FAS, FASLG, GZMB, HAVCR2, IFNG, IL10, IL10RA, IL21, KIR2DL1, KIR2DL2, KIR2DL3, KIR2DS1, KIR2DS2, KIR3D
L1, KIR3DL2, KLRK1, LGALS9, MICA, MICB, PDCD1, PDCD1LG2, PRF1, TGFB1, TGFBR1, TNF, TNFRSF1A, ULBP1, ULBP2

### Heuristics (exact):
- KIR+CD8+: (logexpr CD8A>0.5 or CD8B>0.5) AND any KIR gene logexpr>0.
- Pathogenic CD4+: (logexpr CD4>0.5) AND any activation gene (IFNG,GZMB,PRF1,IL21,TNF,CXCL13) detected.

### Quantitative intermediate results (this run):
- Files processed: 10
- Extracted genes: 37
- Cells after removing zero-sum cells: 0
- KIR+CD8+ cells detected: 0
- Putative pathogenic CD4+ cells detected: 0

### Limitations
- To avoid memory limits, normalization used only the selected gene set rather than genome-wide counts; this can bias expression magnitudes.
- Population definitions are heuristic and lack tetramer/TCR confirmation of gliadin-specificity.
- Scoring is a simple product of mean log-expressions and lacks statistical testing.

## Results
Top LR candidates saved in lr_results.csv (ranked by product of mean log-expression).

## References
- Nagata S. (1997). Apoptosis by death factor. Cell. DOI:10.1016/S0092-8674(00)81849-5
- Voskoboinik I. et al. (2015). Perforin and granzymes. Nat Rev Immunol. DOI:10.1038/nri3839

{
  "analysis": "From the outputs: the header contains quoted cell barcodes (we previously saw sequences like "AACTCTT..."), and header NF=6858 while data lines NF=6859, indicating data lines include an extra first field (likely gene name) that the header lacks. {
  "analysis": "The file's first-column gene identifiers are quoted strings (e.g., "RPS6KA1"), so my earlier streaming parser compared unquoted gene names to quoted tokens and failed to match, producing zero cells. The header barcodes are also quoted. The fix is straightforward: when parsing lines, strip surrounding quotes from gene names and barcode tokens before matching or using them. I'll create a corrected script that strips quotes from gene and barcode tokens, re-run the streaming extraction, recompute the normalized log-expression on the selected genes, and recompute LR scores. This should produce non-zero mean expressions and non-zero LR scores, if the genes are present in the data.",
  "plan": "1) Create a corrected script /app/run_stream_analysis_fix.py that streams gzipped files, strips surrounding double quotes from gene names and barcodes, extracts required genes, concatenates, normalizes over the selected genes, defines populations, computes mean log-expression, scores curated non-MHC-I LR pairs, and writes lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, and /app/answer.txt. 2) Execute the script with the venv Python. 3) Display the generated lr_results.csv, head of /app/trace.md, and /app/answer.txt to verify results.

The new script will explicitly call .strip('"') on gene and barcode tokens and will otherwise follow the previous logic and heuristics.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/run_stream_analysis_fix.py << 'PY'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Fixed streaming analysis: strip surrounding quotes from gene names and barcodes when parsing.
"""
import glob
import gzip
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

files = sorted(glob.glob('/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz'))
print('Found files:', len(files))
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Genes of interest
kir_genes = ['KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2']
marker_genes = ['CD8A','CD8B','CD4']
activation_genes = ['IFNG','GZMB','PRF1','IL21','TNF','CXCL13']
lr_genes = ['FASLG','FAS','CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','TNF','TNFRSF1A','CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2','KLRK1','GZMB']
required_genes = sorted(set(kir_genes + marker_genes + activation_genes + lr_genes))
required_set = set(required_genes)
print('Required genes count:', len(required_genes))

per_sample_dfs = []
for fpath in files:
    sample = fpath.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
    print('Processing', sample)
    with gzip.open(fpath,'rt') as fh:
        header_line = fh.readline().rstrip('\n')
        header_tokens = header_line.split('\t')
        # strip quotes from header tokens
        header_tokens = [t.strip('"') for t in header_tokens]
        # assume first token is empty or 'gene' label; barcodes are after the first token
        barcodes = header_tokens[1:]
        rows = []
        genes_idx = []
        for line in fh:
            parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t')
            if not parts:
                continue
            gene_token = parts[0].strip('"')
            if gene_token in required_set:
                vals = parts[1:]
                if len(vals) != len(barcodes):
                    # skip malformed
                    continue
                rows.append([int(x) if x!='' else 0 for x in vals])
                genes_idx.append(gene_token)
    if not rows:
        print('No required genes found in', sample)
        df = pd.DataFrame(index=required_genes)
    else:
        mat = pd.DataFrame(rows, index=genes_idx, columns=barcodes)
        # if duplicated gene rows, keep first
        mat = mat.groupby(mat.index).first()
        # ensure all required genes present as rows
        mat = mat.reindex(required_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)
        # prefix columns with sample
        mat.columns = [f"{sample}__{c}" for c in mat.columns]
        df = mat
    print('Sample', sample, 'extracted shape (genes x cells):', df.shape)
    per_sample_dfs.append(df)

# concatenate
counts = pd.concat(per_sample_dfs, axis=1)
print('Concatenated counts shape (genes x cells):', counts.shape)

# transpose to cells x genes
cells = counts.T
cells['sample'] = [c.split('__')[0] for c in cells.index]
# remove zero-sum cells across selected genes
nonzero = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
cells = cells.loc[nonzero > 0]
print('Cells after removing zero-sum cells:', cells.shape[0])

# normalize per cell across selected genes and log1p
sums = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).sum(axis=1)
sums[sums==0] = 1
normed = cells.drop(columns=['sample']).div(sums, axis=0) * 1e4
logexpr = np.log1p(normed)
print('Computed logexpr shape:', logexpr.shape)

# heuristics
cd8_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD8A' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8A'] > 0.5)
if 'CD8B' in logexpr.columns:
    cd8_mask = cd8_mask | (logexpr['CD8B'] > 0.5)
kir_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for k in kir_genes:
    if k in logexpr.columns:
        kir_mask = kir_mask | (logexpr[k] > 0.0)
kir_cd8_mask = cd8_mask & kir_mask
print('KIR+CD8+ cells (heuristic):', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

cd4_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
if 'CD4' in logexpr.columns:
    cd4_mask = (logexpr['CD4'] > 0.5)
act_mask = pd.Series(False, index=logexpr.index)
for a in activation_genes:
    if a in logexpr.columns:
        act_mask = act_mask | (logexpr[a] > 0.0)
pathogenic_cd4_mask = cd4_mask & act_mask
print('Pathogenic CD4+ cells (heuristic):', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# fallback relaxed presence-based if empty
if kir_cd8_mask.sum() == 0:
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd8 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD8A' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8A'] > 0)
    if 'CD8B' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd8 = raw_cd8 | (raw['CD8B'] > 0)
    raw_kir = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for k in kir_genes:
        if k in raw.columns:
            raw_kir = raw_kir | (raw[k] > 0)
    kir_cd8_mask = raw_cd8 & raw_kir
    print('After relaxing, KIR+CD8+ count:', int(kir_cd8_mask.sum()))

if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum() == 0:
    raw = counts.T
    raw_cd4 = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    if 'CD4' in raw.columns:
        raw_cd4 = raw_cd4 | (raw['CD4'] > 0)
    raw_act = pd.Series(False, index=raw.index)
    for a in activation_genes:
        if a in raw.columns:
            raw_act = raw_act | (raw[a] > 0)
    pathogenic_cd4_mask = raw_cd4 & raw_act
    print('After relaxing, pathogenic CD4+ count:', int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()))

# mean vectors
mean_kir = logexpr.loc[kir_cd8_mask].mean(axis=0) if kir_cd8_mask.sum()>0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)
mean_path = logexpr.loc[pathogenic_cd4_mask].mean(axis=0) if pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum()>0 else pd.Series(0, index=logexpr.columns)

# LR pairs
lr_pairs = [
    ('FASLG','FAS','Fas-FasL (apoptosis)'),
    ('CD274','PDCD1','PD-L1:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('PDCD1LG2','PDCD1','PD-L2:PD-1 (inhibitory)'),
    ('LGALS9','HAVCR2','Galectin-9:TIM-3 (inhibitory)'),
    ('TNF','TNFRSF1A','TNF:TNFRSF1A (inflammatory/apoptotic)'),
    ('CCL5','CCR5','CCL5:CCR5 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('CXCL9','CXCR3','CXCL9:CXCR3 (chemotaxis)'),
    ('IL10','IL10RA','IL-10:IL10R (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('TGFB1','TGFBR1','TGF-beta:TGFBR (immunosuppressive)'),
    ('MICA','KLRK1','MICA:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('MICB','KLRK1','MICB:NKG2D (stress/NKG2D activation)'),
    ('ULBP1','KLRK1','ULBP1:NKG2D'),
    ('ULBP2','KLRK1','ULBP2:NKG2D'),
    ('GZMB','FAS','Granzyme B -> apoptosis (cytotoxic effector)')
]
rows = []
for lig, rec, note in lr_pairs:
    lig_mean = float(mean_kir.get(lig, 0.0))
    rec_mean = float(mean_path.get(rec, 0.0))
    score = lig_mean * rec_mean
    rows.append({'ligand': lig, 'receptor': rec, 'note': note, 'ligand_mean_logexpr': lig_mean, 'receptor_mean_logexpr': rec_mean, 'score': score})

res = pd.DataFrame(rows).sort_values('score', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
res.to_csv('lr_results.csv', index=False)
print('Wrote lr_results.csv')

# write trace and answer
with open('/app/trace.md','w') as f:
    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')
    f.write('Summary: fixed parsing to strip quotes from gene and barcode tokens; ran streaming extraction and LR scoring.\n')
    f.write(f'Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {cells.shape[0]}\n')
    f.write(f'KIR+CD8+ cells (heuristic): {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n')
    f.write(f'Pathogenic CD4+ cells (heuristic): {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n')

with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('Predicted top non-MHC-I signaling pathways mediating KIR+CD8+ T cell suppression and cytotoxic targeting of pathogenic gliadin-reactive CD4+ T cells:\n\n')
    top = res[res['score']>0].head(8)
    if top.empty:
        f.write('No non-zero scoring ligand-receptor pairs were detected with the curated list and heuristic definitions.\n')
    else:
        for _, row in top.iterrows():
            f.write(f"- {row['note']} (ligand {row['ligand']} in KIR+CD8+; receptor {row['receptor']} in pathogenic CD4+). Score={row['score']:.4g}\n")
    f.write('\nInterpretation: see trace.md for details.\n')

print('Finished fixed streaming analysis')
PY

    },

    f.write('# Analysis Trace for DA-11-1\n\n')

    f.write(f'Files processed: {len(files)}\n')
    f.write(f'Cells after removing zero-sum cells: {cells.shape[0]}\n')
    f.write(f'KIR+CD8+ cells (heuristic): {int(kir_cd8_mask.sum())}\n')
    f.write(f'Pathogenic CD4+ cells (heuristic): {int(pathogenic_cd4_mask.sum())}\n')

with open('/app/answer.txt','w') as f:
    f.write('Predicted top non-MHC-I signaling pathways mediating KIR+CD8+ T cell suppression and cytotoxic targeting of pathogenic gliadin-reactive CD4+ T cells:\n\n')
    top = res[res['score']>0].head(8)
    if top.empty:
        f.write('No non-zero scoring ligand-receptor pairs were detected with the curated list and heuristic definitions.\n')
    else:
        for _, row in top.iterrows():
            f.write(f"- {row['note']} (ligand {row['ligand']} in KIR+CD8+; receptor {row['receptor']} in pathogenic CD4+). Score={row['score']:.4g}\n")
    f.write('\nInterpretation: see trace.md for details.\n')

print('Finished fixed streaming analysis')
PY
", "duration": 0.1}

content.text:
{
  "analysis": "So far I explored the 10 gzipped count matrices under /app/data/GSE193442_RAW. I created a venv and installed pandas/numpy/scikit-learn, and iteratively wrote streaming parsers to avoid loading full matrices. Files are quoted (gene names and barcodes are surrounded by double quotes). My earlier parsing initially failed due to quoting and an off-by-one mismatch between header and data line token counts; I fixed those issues. Despite that, the streaming analysis reported 0 cells with non-zero sums across the small curated gene set, and all LR scores were zero. Inspection of the gene names in the file showed a list of many genes but I did not find canonical T cell markers (CD4, CD8A/B, CD3 family) in the sample subset I examined. This indicates either those canonical markers are absent in these matrices or appear under different names/aliases, or there remain parsing edge cases.

What has been accomplished:
- Located all 10 gzipped count files.
- Confirmed file format: first line header contains quoted barcodes; subsequent lines have quoted gene names in first column followed by numeric counts. Some lines originally had token-count mismatches which I made the parser tolerant of.
- Implemented streaming extraction scripts that safely extract required genes, handling quoted tokens and mismatched token counts.
- Produced preliminary outputs (lr_results.csv, /app/trace.md, /app/answer.txt), but they contained zero-results because no cells had non-zero sums across the selected gene list.

What still needs to be done:
- Determine which immune / marker genes are actually present in the matrices (search for a wide panel of markers) and whether those genes have non-zero counts.
- Based on which markers are present, define KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ populations (or reasonable proxies), then compute ligand-receptor scores between sender (KIR+CD8+) and receiver (pathogenic CD4+) using the streaming approach.

I will now search all files for occurrences of immune marker names and KIR genes (using quoted tokens), and build a compact table of which of our candidate marker/ligand/receptor genes are present across the 10 files. This will let us choose marker definitions that actually exist in the data (or conclude that the dataset lacks the required markers for the requested cell-type calls).",
  "plan": "1) Create a small Python script (/app/scan_gene_presence.py) that streams every gzipped count file and records which gene names (first column, stripped of quotes) appear in each file. The script will focus on a comprehensive list of candidate genes (canonical markers, KIRs, ligands, receptors). It will produce a table (CSV) of presence/absence across the 10 samples and a summary listing of any matching genes. 2) Run the script with the venv Python and inspect the presence table and matches. 3) Based on the results, choose an approach: (a) if canonical markers (CD8A, KIRs, CD4, activation genes) are present, re-run the streaming LR analysis using those; (b) if they are absent, choose alternative marker genes present in the data and document the limitation in the trace.

The next commands will create and run the scanning script and print its outputs. This is a diagnostic step required before we can produce a valid inference of cell-cell interactions.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/scan_gene_presence.py << 'PY'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Scan genes present in each gzipped count matrix (first column = gene name, quoted).
Writes /app/gene_presence.csv (rows: gene, cols: sample basename -> 0/1 presence) and /app/gene_presence_summary.txt
"""
import glob
import gzip
import csv
from collections import defaultdict

files = sorted(glob.glob('/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz'))
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Candidate genes to look for (expanded list):
candidate_genes = [
    'CD4','CD8A','CD8B','CD3D','CD3E','CD3G','TRAC','TRBC1','TRBC2',
    'KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2',
    'FAS','FASLG','GZMB','GZMA','PRF1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2',
    'CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1',
    'CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','KLRK1','KLRB1','NKG2D'
]
# also track all observed genes (sample of many)
observed = defaultdict(set)
presence = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int))

for fpath in files:
    sample = fpath.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
    print('Scanning', sample)
    with gzip.open(fpath, 'rt') as fh:
        header = fh.readline()  # skip header
        for i, line in enumerate(fh):
            # parse first token (gene), remove surrounding quotes and whitespace
            parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t',1)
            if not parts:
                continue
            gene = parts[0].strip().strip('"')
            observed[sample].add(gene)
            if gene in candidate_genes:
                presence[gene][sample] = 1
            # limit scanning speed: we can scan entire file; files are large but streaming is OK
    print('  unique genes observed in', sample, ':', len(observed[sample]))

# produce union of genes from observed sets (sample a subset)
union_obs = set().union(*observed.values())
print('Total unique genes observed across all files (sample):', len(union_obs))

# write presence table for candidate_genes
out_csv = '/app/gene_presence.csv'
with open(out_csv, 'w', newline='') as outf:
    writer = csv.writer(outf)
    header = ['gene'] + [f.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','') for f in files]
    writer.writerow(header)
    for g in candidate_genes:
        row = [g]
        for f in files:
            s = f.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
            row.append(presence[g].get(s, 0))
        writer.writerow(row)

# write summary of candidate genes present
with open('/app/gene_presence_summary.txt','w') as s:
    s.write('Candidate genes presence across samples:\n')

{"keystrokes": "cat > /app/scan_gene_presence.py << 'PY'
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Scan genes present in each gzipped count matrix (first column = gene name, quoted).
Writes /app/gene_presence.csv (rows: gene, cols: sample basename -> 0/1 presence) and /app/gene_presence_summary.txt
"""
import glob
import gzip
import csv
from collections import defaultdict

files = sorted(glob.glob('/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/*.txt.gz'))
if not files:
    raise SystemExit('No files found')

# Candidate genes to look for (expanded list):
candidate_genes = [
    'CD4','CD8A','CD8B','CD3D','CD3E','CD3G','TRAC','TRBC1','TRBC2',
    'KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2',
    'FAS','FASLG','GZMB','GZMA','PRF1','MICA','MICB','ULBP1','ULBP2',
    'CD274','PDCD1','PDCD1LG2','LGALS9','HAVCR2','IL10','IL10RA','TGFB1','TGFBR1',
    'CCL5','CCR5','CXCL9','CXCR3','KLRK1','KLRB1','NKG2D'
]
# also track all observed genes (sample of many)
observed = defaultdict(set)
presence = defaultdict(lambda: defaultdict(int))

for fpath in files:
    sample = fpath.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
    print('Scanning', sample)
    with gzip.open(fpath, 'rt') as fh:
        header = fh.readline()  # skip header
        for i, line in enumerate(fh):
            # parse first token (gene), remove surrounding quotes and whitespace
            parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t',1)
            if not parts:
                continue
            gene = parts[0].strip().strip('"')
            observed[sample].add(gene)
            if gene in candidate_genes:
                presence[gene][sample] = 1
            # limit scanning speed: we can scan entire file; files are large but streaming is OK
    print('  unique genes observed in', sample, ':', len(observed[sample]))

# produce union of genes from observed sets (sample a subset)
union_obs = set().union(*observed.values())
print('Total unique genes observed across all files (sample):', len(union_obs))

# write presence table for candidate_genes
out_csv = '/app/gene_presence.csv'
with open(out_csv, 'w', newline='') as outf:
    writer = csv.writer(outf)
    header = ['gene'] + [f.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','') for f in files]
    writer.writerow(header)
    for g in candidate_genes:
        row = [g]
        for f in files:
            s = f.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
            row.append(presence[g].get(s, 0))
        writer.writerow(row)

# write summary of candidate genes present
with open('/app/gene_presence_summary.txt','w') as s:
    s.write('Candidate genes presence across samples:\n')
    for g in candidate_genes:
        counts = sum(presence[g].values())
        s.write(f"{g}: present in {counts} / {len(files)} samples\n")
    s.write('\nSample gene counts (observed unique genes per sample):\n')
    for f in files:
        sname = f.split('/')[-1].replace('.txt.gz','')
        s.write(f"{sname}: {len(observed[sname])}\n")

print('Wrote', out_csv, 'and /app/gene_presence_summary.txt')
PY
", "duration": 0.1}

That means the matrices do contain the relevant genes and the earlier zero-results are due to parsing/alignment issues when extracting numeric counts per cell (not due to gene absence). I need to inspect the exact alignment between header barcodes and data-token columns for a representative sample (GSM5820724) and a few representative genes (GZMB, KIR3DL1, CD8B) to determine why the streaming script produced zero-sum cells. Specifically, I want to confirm: header token count, data line token count, how numeric tokens are formatted (are they quoted?), and whether a simple conversion to int yields nonzero values for those genes. Once I confirm the token layout and numeric formatting, I will re-run the robust streaming extraction (or fix it) to compute per-cell sums and identify KIR+CD8+ and pathogenic CD4+ cells.

So far: data files located and gene presence confirmed. Remaining: diagnose the parser mismatch by printing header and sample gene lines and numeric token parsing behavior.",
  "plan": "1) Use a short Python probe that opens the first sample gzipped file, reads the header and prints: number of header tokens, first 12 barcode tokens with quotes removed. 2) Locate specific gene lines (GZMB, CD8B, KIR3DL1) and print for each: number of tokens in the full line, number of value tokens (parts[1:]), compare to header length, print the first 40 value tokens (repr and as ints after stripping quotes) and show sum and count of nonzero values. 3) Use the results to determine whether numeric tokens are quoted and whether any alignment adjustments (taking last N tokens, stripping quotes) produce non-zero integers. If the probe shows non-zero integers with proper alignment, re-run the streaming script (it already contains tolerant logic); if not, adapt further.

I will run the probe now on GSM5820724 and check GZMB, CD8B, and KIR3DL1 lines.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - << 'PY'
import gzip
path = '/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820724_MS01092019_counts.txt.gz'
genes_to_check = ['GZMB','CD8B','KIR3DL1']
print('File:', path)
with gzip.open(path,'rt') as fh:
    header = fh.readline().rstrip('\n')
    hparts = header.split('\t')
    print('Header tokens:', len(hparts))
    print('First 12 barcodes (stripped):')
    for t in hparts[1:13]:
        print(' ', t.strip('"'))
    # build a small index for quick lookup
    fh_pos = fh.tell()
    found = {g: None for g in genes_to_check}
    for i,line in enumerate(fh, start=1):
        parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t')
        gene = parts[0].strip('"') if parts else ''
        if gene in found and found[gene] is None:
            found[gene] = (i+1, line)  # store line number and content
        if all(v is not None for v in found.values()):
            break
    for g in genes_to_check:
        info = found[g]
        if info is None:
            print(f'{g}: NOT FOUND in first scan')
            continue
        ln, line = info
        parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t')
        vals = parts[1:]
        print('\nGene:', g)
        print('  line_number:', ln)
        print('  n_parts:', len(parts), 'n_vals:', len(vals), 'n_header_tokens:', len(hparts))
        # show first 40 raw value tokens repr
        sample_tokens = vals[:40]
        print('  first 40 raw tokens (repr):')
        print('   ', [repr(t) for t in sample_tokens])
        # attempt to align by taking last N tokens if mismatch
        N = len(hparts)-1
        if len(vals) != N:
            if len(vals) > N:
                aligned = vals[-N:]
                print('  note: vals > header-1; aligning by taking last N tokens')
            else:
                # left-pad with '0'
                aligned = ['0']*(N-len(vals)) + vals
                print('  note: vals < header-1; left-padding with zeros')
        else:
            aligned = vals
        # strip quotes and convert to ints for first 40
        def to_ints(tokens, k=40):
            out = []
            for t in tokens[:k]:
                t2 = t.strip('"')
                try:
                    out.append(int(t2) if t2!='' else 0)
                except:
                    out.append(None)
            return out
        ints = to_ints(aligned, 80)
        nz = sum(1 for x in ints if x and x>0)
        ssum = sum(x for x in ints if isinstance(x,int))
        print('  sample converted ints (first 40):')
        print('   ', ints[:40])
        print('  count_nonzero_in_sample:', nz, 'sum_sample:', ssum)

print('\nProbe complete')
PY
",
      "duration": 3.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}

  "plan": "Run a short Python check that reads one sample (GSM5820724_MS01092019_counts.txt.gz) using the same parsing/alignment/quote-stripping logic as the streaming script and then:
- Print mat.shape (genes x cells), number of columns (cells), list first 10 column names.
- Compute per-cell sums across the selected required_genes and report how many cells have sum>0, and some sample sums.
- Print the extracted row values for CD8B and GZMB (first 40 aligned integer values and their sum) to confirm numeric conversion.
Based on the results, we will either fix the concatenation stage in the full script or re-run the full streaming script after adjusting the parsing. Commands below run the single-sample check and print diagnostics.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - << 'PY'
import gzip
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

path = '/app/data/GSE193442_RAW/GSM5820724_MS01092019_counts.txt.gz'
required_genes = ['KIR2DL1','KIR2DL2','KIR2DL3','KIR3DL1','KIR3DL2','KIR2DS1','KIR2DS2','CD8A','CD8B','CD4','IFNG','GZMB','PRF1','IL21','FASLG','FAS','MICA','MICB','CD274','PDCD1']
required_genes = sorted(set(required_genes))
print('Required genes:', required_genes)
with gzip.open(path,'rt') as fh:
    header = fh.readline().rstrip('\n')
    hparts = [t.strip('"') for t in header.split('\t')]
    barcodes = hparts[1:]
    rows = []
    genes_idx = []
    for line in fh:
        parts = line.rstrip('\n').split('\t')
        if not parts:
            continue
        gene = parts[0].strip('"')
        if gene in required_genes:
            vals = parts[1:]
            N = len(barcodes)
            if len(vals) != N:
                if len(vals) > N:
                    vals = vals[-N:]
                else:
                    vals = ['0']*(N-len(vals)) + vals
            # strip quotes and convert
            clean = [int(x.strip('"')) if x.strip('"')!='' else 0 for x in vals]
            rows.append(clean)
            genes_idx.append(gene)

# Construct DataFrame genes x cells
if rows:
    mat = pd.DataFrame(rows, index=genes_idx, columns=barcodes)
    # ensure ordering and presence of all required_genes
    mat = mat.groupby(mat.index).first()
    mat = mat.reindex(required_genes).fillna(0).astype(int)
else:
    mat = pd.DataFrame(index=required_genes)

print('Sample matrix shape (genes x cells):', mat.shape)
print('Number of cells (columns):', mat.shape[1])
print('First 10 columns:', list(mat.columns[:10]))
# per-cell sums
if mat.shape[1] > 0:
    sums = mat.sum(axis=0)
    nz_cells = (sums>0).sum()
    print('Cells with sum>0:', int(nz_cells), 'out of', mat.shape[1])
    print('Sample per-cell sums (first 20):', list(sums[:20]))
else:
    print('No cells in sample after extraction')

# show CD8B and GZMB rows if present
for g in ['CD8B','GZMB','KIR3DL1']:
    if g in mat.index:
        row = mat.loc[g]
        print('\nGene', g, 'row length:', len(row))
        vals = list(row[:80])

        print(' first 40 vals:', vals[:40])
        print(' nonzero_count (first 80):', nz, 'sum (first 80):', ssum)

        print('\nGene', g, 'not found in extracted mat')
